name: Brigid Shea Campaign
resolved_employer: Campaign committee of Brigid Shea, Travis County Commissioner Precinct 2 (officeholder political committee)
evidence: Not an individual donor: this is the campaign/officeholder committee of Travis County Commissioner Brigid Shea (Precinct 2, elected 2015, environmentalist Democrat). It transferred funds to fellow Travis County Democratic officials Travillion, Howard, and Gomez, which fits a candidate-committee-to-allied-candidates pattern (78757 is her Austin base).

name: Stephens, Juanita
evidence: Individual donor in Cedar Park, TX 78613 who gave $2,000 to Travillion; no occupation or employer reported. 'Juanita Stephens' is a common name and no public source corroborates a specific individual, occupation, or industry. Cannot confidently identify.

name: Canfield, Cash
resolved_employer: American Structurepoint, Inc. (President & partner) — national architecture/engineering consulting firm (probable)
evidence: 'Cash Canfield' is a distinctive, rare name; the prominent bearer is Cash Canfield, President and partner of American Structurepoint (Indianapolis-HQ transportation/civil-engineering firm) which has been aggressively building a Texas transportation practice and courting Texas state/municipal decision-makers. Donations to Travis County Commissioner Travillion (transportation/CAMPO portfolio) plus Howard fit a transportation-engineering exec seeking public infrastructure work. Caveat: his published bio places him in Indianapolis (a colleague, Steven Miller, leads AS's Texas ops), and the 78746 Westlake residential address is not independently confirmed to him — so identity rests on the rare name + donation pattern rather than address.

name: Stephens, Juanita
evidence: Individual donor in Pflugerville, TX 78660 who gave $2,000 to Travillion; no occupation or employer reported. Common name; distinct entry from the Cedar Park 'Juanita Stephens' in this batch. No public source corroborates a specific person, occupation, or industry. Cannot confidently identify.

name: Ironworkers State COPE Fund
resolved_employer: Iron Workers union — state Committee on Political Education (COPE) political action fund
evidence: Labor union PAC: the Ironworkers (International Association of Bridge, Structural, Ornamental and Reinforcing Iron Workers) state Committee on Political Education fund, a union political-education/PAC vehicle (Grapevine, TX 76051 address). Made contributions to Travillion and Shea, consistent with organized-labor support for Travis County Democrats.

name: Rhone, Michael
evidence: Public records place a Michael C. Rhone (b. ~1965) at 803 Sykes Ct, Pflugerville, TX 78660-8008 — matching the donor's exact ZIP+4 (78660-8008), so identity is fairly well corroborated. However, occupation/industry is not reliably established (aggregator data is inconsistent; a Michael Rhone also appears as plaintiff in a suit against the City of Austin/APD). No confident industry classification.

name: Dejoria, Eloise
resolved_employer: Self / philanthropist; actress-producer (Weekend at Bernie's); wife of billionaire John Paul DeJoria (Paul Mitchell, Patrón, oil)
evidence: Eloise Broady DeJoria — model, actress/film producer, and philanthropist, wife of billionaire entrepreneur John Paul DeJoria (co-founder of Paul Mitchell and Patrón). The couple lives in Austin (78733 is the Westlake/Bee Cave area), and her self-reported occupation 'Philanthropist / Self' matches her public profile of charitable work (Austin Recovery, Long Center, Austin Film Society). Gave $2,000 to Travis County Judge Andy Brown.

affiliations.org: Skidmore Energy / Maghreb Petroleum Exploration (Morocco oil)
affiliations.role: spouse of oil-and-gas investor John Paul DeJoria, who was a major investor in Skidmore Energy and its Moroccan oil-exploration venture

name: Wasek, Don
resolved_employer: Buc-ee's, Ltd. (co-founder / equal partner) — Texas travel-convenience-store & fuel chain
evidence: 'Don Wasek' is the distinctive name of the co-founder and equal partner of Buc-ee's (with Arch 'Beaver' Aplin III) — the large Texas travel-center/convenience-store and fuel retail chain; Wasek runs operations. He is a known Texas Republican megadonor, consistent with this record's fully Republican federal-donation lean (0.0). No ZIP was provided to fully lock identity, but the rare name + Republican donor profile fit. Gave $2,000 to Travillion and Howard.

name: Stuart, Carl
resolved_employer: Carl Stuart Investment Advisor, Inc. / Stuart Investment Advisors — founder & president; host of 'Money Talk' radio (KUT)
evidence: Carl Stuart is a longtime Austin fee-based investment advisor (~47 years), founder/president of Stuart Investment Advisors (~$515M AUM) and host of 'Money Talk,' Austin's longest-running financial call-in radio show. He is a well-known Austin civic figure (boards of Zach Theatre, Austin Smiles). 78703 (central/west Austin) fits his Austin base; gave to Travis County Commissioner Ann Howard.

name: Gilliland, Douglas
evidence: Downtown-Austin (78701) donor, prolific (72 federal donations, fully Republican-leaning) who nonetheless gave ~$1,785 to Democratic Commissioner Travillion. Two candidate 'Doug/Douglas Gilliland' profiles exist but neither is confirmed to this record: (1) Doug Gilliland, President of Taurus of Texas (the FIREARMS maker Taurus) — but he is based in Roanoke, TX, not Austin, and a gun-industry exec repeatedly funding a Democrat does not fit; (2) a 'Douglas H. Gilliland' slug once appeared under Taurus Investment Holdings (a real-estate private-equity firm with an Austin office), which would fit a 78701 real-estate professional, but he is not currently listed there and cannot be verified. Identity unresolved — no industry assigned.
